name: pdf_tools
short_description: PDF manipulation and Markdown conversion toolkit for extracting page ranges and converting PDFs to Markdown.
long_description: pdf_tools is a Python library and command-line tool for working with PDF files. It provides utilities to extract single pages or multiple page ranges from PDF documents, count pages, and convert PDF content to Markdown format. The toolkit combines PyPDF2 for PDF manipulation with MarkItDown for intelligent format conversion, making it easy to programmatically process PDFs or quickly extract content from the command line. It's useful for document processing pipelines, content extraction, PDF splitting, and converting PDF documents to editable Markdown.

modules.description: Core PDFTools class providing page counting, range extraction, and Markdown conversion from PDF pages using PyPDF2 and MarkItDown.
Command-line interface exposing PDF operations including count, extract, and markdown conversion commands with argument parsing.
Package initialization exposing PDFTools class and type aliases (PdfPath, PageRange) for public API.
Entry point for running the package as a module with python -m pdf_tools.
Test suite for PDFTools covering page counting, range extraction, and Markdown conversion functionality.

author: Philip Orange
